extractedFrom: phase1-aks-automatic-grounding.md Section 5.2 (R1-R17) + Phase 1.6 simulation findings (R18+ + named patterns)
promotionPolicy: promotion_candidate: true means recipe appears in 5+ sims and may be worth promoting to a first-class rich component in Phase 3 (vs composing from primitives every time).

recipes.name: Plan summary card
recipes.intent: Single-card plan summary for floor-case (single-container, single-deployable)
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2)+Text(body)+List+Row[Button × 2]]
fires_when: track:containerized_web with single service AND no existing manifests
anti_patterns: use R2 if ≥2 distinct concepts (cost, preview, identity wiring)

recipes.name: Multi-card plan
recipes.intent: Multiple sibling Cards in a Column when plan has >5 distinct concepts that benefit from independent objection
recipes.composition: Column[Card(plan)+Card(invisible-work)+Card(cost)+Row[Button × N]]
fires_when: multi-service web
agentic app with multiple integration points
greenfield architecture proposal
migration with multiple phases
anti_patterns: use R1 if simple single-deployable
do NOT collapse into one mega-card — defeats the independent-objection model

recipes.name: Multi-card with hierarchical inner content
recipes.intent: Each Card in R2 column gets defined inner structure with 2-3 group headers and visual separators
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2)+Group(IN-CLUSTER WORKLOADS)+Group(MANAGED AZURE RESOURCES)+Group(PLATFORM)] using ╭─...─╮ framing + ▸ markers + ★ for highlights
fires_when: R2 inner Card has >5 items
anti_patterns: flat inner List for >5 items reads as a wall

recipes.name: Migration mapping table (PaaS source → Azure equivalent)
recipes.intent: Mental-model bridge for PaaS migrants — opening surface BEFORE any plan card
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 source→target headline)+Table[from_source|to_azure|why_this_thing]+Text(small, citation)]
fires_when: intent:paas_migration with named source (render, heroku, vercel, fly, netlify, railway)
anti_patterns: do NOT emit plan card before R3 — bridges mental model first

recipes.name: Per-item cards in Column with bulk + per-item actions
recipes.intent: Multiple per-app cards plus master 'approve all N' affordance for bulk scenarios
recipes.composition: Column[Text(h2)+Card(item-1)+Card(item-2)+...+Card(rollup)+Row[Button(approve-all)+Button(one-at-a-time)]]
fires_when: bulk mode with ≥2 distinct items needing per-item plans
anti_patterns: force user through serial 1-by-1 review when bulk is the intent

recipes.name: Diff plan (additive, with markers)
recipes.intent: Show what's preserved/added/modified/deleted before any file write
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2)+List with leading '+ '/'  '/'- '/'~ ' markers per line + Text(annotation column)+Row[Button(approve)]]
fires_when: existing files in scope (existing manifests, existing GHA workflows, iteration mode)
anti_patterns: overwrite without showing diff first
merge unchanged + changed in same list without markers
recipes.notes: Marker set: + (new file), ~ (modified, with line-count delta), - (deleted), space (unchanged). The ~ marker was surfaced by sim-09.

recipes.name: Why this stack (rationale card)
recipes.intent: Greenfield architecture rationale — why each major decision was made
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+List of one-line reasons tied to user's situation]
fires_when: greenfield architecture proposal
user asks 'why X?'
anti_patterns: abstract general reasons (use Microsoft Learn doc page instead) — must be tied to user's specific situation

recipes.name: What I'm doing for you (reassurance list)
recipes.intent: Surface invisible work the system handles when user is M1/M2 (no infra expertise)
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'What I'm doing for you')+List of specific items tied to user's artifacts]
fires_when: persona is M1/M2/M3
user said 'I don't really do K8s' / 'just deploy this'
anti_patterns: generic items like 'setting up your cluster' — must be specific to user's actual files / services / inferred dependencies (validated sim-03 S3)

recipes.name: What each artifact does FOR you (3-column comparison)
recipes.intent: When user pushes back on the visible weight of files AND has named a comparator product, show 3-column: File / What X did invisibly / What this file buys you
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+Table[File|What_comparator_did|What_this_file_buys_you]+Text(small)]
fires_when: user pushed back on file count AND named comparator (Vercel, Heroku, Render)
anti_patterns: abstract comparison without naming the user's actual files

recipes.name: Job-to-be-done table (you want / how)
recipes.intent: When user names a competing product OR pushes on cost, parse JTBD and address each — without recommending the named product back
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 'Same outcome — let me reshape')+Table[You_want|How_AKS_Automatic_does_it_honest]+Row[Button(reshape)+Button(stay)+Button(honest_exit)]]
fires_when: user named Container Apps / App Service / Functions / non-Azure compute
user pushed back on cost
anti_patterns: recommend the named alternative back (violates Section 6.6)
lead with 'Here's why AKS Automatic is better than X' — frame as reshape, not defense

recipes.name: Review pack composition
recipes.intent: Package multi-file review pack for human SRE/ops reviewer
recipes.composition: Column[Card(contents-with-✨/⚙ provenance tags)+Card(delivery-options Row[Button × N])]
fires_when: intent:handover to human reviewer
user said 'package up for review' / 'SRE review' / 'send to <name>'
anti_patterns: dump file list (curate reading order)
single-channel delivery (offer PR / Slack / ZIP / link / terminal)

recipes.name: File-preview Card (sub-pattern of R9)
recipes.intent: Preview a generated file with first ~10-12 lines + 'see full file' affordance, optionally with per-line annotations
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'filename (preview)')+CodeBlock(first ~10-12 lines)+Text(small, 'see full file →')] OR Card[Table[line|content|annotation]] for annotated previews
fires_when: any 'show me what you generated' flow
review-pack delivery
before approval of large diff
anti_patterns: dump full file inline (D1 — codesmith output goes to surfaces)

recipes.name: Single grounded question with citation
recipes.intent: Single-question RadioGroup or prose question with source citation; replaces multi-field Questionnaire when only one ambiguity matters
recipes.composition: Card[Text(p reflection)+Text(p grounding)+RadioGroup[option × N]+Text(small, 'Source: <tool/skill citation>')]
fires_when: exactly one ambiguity remains AND it can be enumerated as 2-5 options
anti_patterns: multi-field Questionnaire when only 1 ambiguity (sim-03 S4)
no source citation for a question that came from a tool result

recipes.name: Compatibility scorecard (4-bucket severity Table)
recipes.intent: Migration / readiness scorecard with 4 severity buckets: incompatible / requiresChanges / autoFixed / informational
recipes.composition: Column[Text(h1)+Card(summary Table 4-bucket)+Card(per-manifest breakdown)+Text(small, 'Source: constraint spec v<x.y.z> AKS <date>')]
fires_when: intent:migration_readiness
agent:aks.reviewer in readiness mode
anti_patterns: flat severity list (loses bucketing)
missing spec version citation
for templated sources (Helm/Kustomize), missing 'fixes write back to chart, not render' framing

recipes.name: Auto-fix informational card (mutator transparency)
recipes.intent: Surface what AKS Automatic mutates at admission with the EXACT injected YAML; offer 'leave as-is' or 'add to my YAML explicitly' (D10)
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, '🔧 Auto-fixes — informational')+List(per-mutator: name+description+exact injected YAML)+Row[Button(leave_as_is)+Button(make_explicit)]]
fires_when: any safeguard scorecard with autoFixed bucket non-empty
any plan that has resource requests but no limits (mutator will fire)
anti_patterns: paraphrase the mutator behavior (cite spec verbatim)
force explicit when assessment is read-only mode

recipes.name: Combined diff with single approval
recipes.intent: Bulk-fix pattern from readiness skill: per-line [Deterministic] / [LLM-reasoned] badges; single TextField for LLM-reasoned input; one approve button
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+List of fixes with badges+per-file diff blocks+TextField(for LLM-reasoned input)+Row[Button(approve)+Button(one_at_a_time)+Button(copy_diff)]]
fires_when: ≥2 requiresChanges findings can be batched
user has selected redesign options for incompatible findings
anti_patterns: one approve per fix (forces multi-turn approval) when batchable
no Deterministic/LLM-reasoned distinction

recipes.name: Capacity check card
recipes.intent: Quota preflight before SKU recommendation
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'Capacity check — <region>')+Table[resource|required|available|status]+Text(small, source citation)+Row[Button(use_alternative)+Button(request_quota)+Button(pick_region)]]
fires_when: any plan that needs vCPUs / GPUs / IPs / unusual VM families (D13)
anti_patterns: recommend SKU before checking quota
ask user 'GPU preference' instead of reflex-checking

recipes.name: Cost card (live retail prices)
recipes.intent: Reflex-included cost card in every plan card per D14
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+Table[Resource|SKU|Qty|Unit|Monthly]+Text(p, fixed-vs-variable annotation)+Text(small, 'Live prices via Azure Retail Prices API')]
fires_when: EVERY plan card emission (universal invariant per all 11 sims)
anti_patterns: one cost number without line items
missing fixed-vs-variable annotation
stale prices (must call azure.pricing_lookup)
recipes.notes: Surfaced extensions: per-PR-day breakdown (sim-05), per-app + shared-infra subtotal (sim-11), idle-vs-active GPU lines separated (sim-03), source-platform-comparison column (sim-02, sim-10, sim-11), per-line negotiation hints in handover mode (sim-12).

recipes.name: Cost card with comparison column (was/now/Δ/what changed)
recipes.intent: Re-emit cost card after a user-driven reshape, NOT when emitting fresh
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+Table[Resource|SKU|Was|Now|Δ|What_changed]+Text(p, summary delta)]
fires_when: user pushed back on cost; system reshaped to deliver same outcome cheaper
applied trim follow-ups
anti_patterns: use for fresh cost cards (use R16 instead)

recipes.name: Cost-trim with compliance-load-bearing column
recipes.intent: When user pushes back on cost AND has stated a non-cost constraint, show what's negotiable vs load-bearing
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+Table[Line|Cost|What_it_pays_for|Can_we_cut?]+Card(trims_with_savings)+Text(p, 'trimmed total: $X')+Text(p, honest 'this is the floor' sentence)]
fires_when: user pushed back on cost AND complianceMode/scaleMode/audienceMode is true
anti_patterns: mark every line as 'fat' (defeats the framing)
auto-apply trims without user opt-in

recipes.name: Next-surface handover (closing card)
recipes.intent: Universal closing card with deep-linked surfaces; replaces 'let me know if you have questions'
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'Where to next')+Row[Button(vscode.dev)+Button(Codespace)+Button(local_VS_Code)+Button(Azure_Portal)]+Card('What you can do next' List with icons+links)+Card('Artifacts you got' List with file links)+optional Card(futureYouDoors)]
fires_when: EVERY conversation that produced an artifact, deployment, or state-change (universal invariant per all 11 sims)
anti_patterns: end with 'let me know if you have questions'
LLM-generated URLs (use core.build_handover_urls Phase 3 tool to avoid encoding bugs)

missing per-scenario tailoring (inference: Codespace+curl pre-loaded; migration: Phase-2 resume hook; iteration: pre-filtered cluster blade + rollback affordance; handover: 'if reviewer requests changes' iteration loop)

extension_slots.futureYouDoors: List of {prompt, action, description} doors the user can walk through later. Surfaced by sim-03, sim-05, sim-09, sim-10, sim-12.

recipes.name: Cross-artifact dependency check
recipes.intent: Before approving a patch to artifact A, identify artifact B that depends on A's contract; inspect B; surface cross-artifact concern
recipes.composition: (approval flag in R14 card) — flag B-might-break in approval prose, offer A-fix-only OR A+B-fix bundles
fires_when: patch on manifest references Dockerfile (USER, EXPOSE, CMD)
patch on Bicep references resource that another module uses
patch on HTTPRoute references hostname that Gateway listener uses
anti_patterns: approve in isolation (sim-02 — runAsUser:1000 + Dockerfile USER root would CrashLoop)

recipes.name: Honest substitution card
recipes.intent: When system swaps a default driven by a constraint (quota, cost, compliance, capability gap), surface the swap reasoning
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'Recommended <substitute>')+Text(p, 'I was going to suggest <default> but <constraint>. <Substitute> works because: <bullets>. You also gain: <benefit>. You give up: <tradeoff>.')+Text(small, 'If <constraint> goes away, switch back: <how>')]
fires_when: quota=0 for default SKU (sim-03)
user push-back on cost with named trigger (sim-05)
compliance forces a tier choice (sim-10)
anti_patterns: silent substitution
blame the user for the constraint

recipes.name: Cold-start breakdown (4-phase timing table)
recipes.intent: Breakdown of where cold-start time goes for KEDA scale-to-zero on stateful workloads
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3)+OrderedList(4 phases with timings)+Text(p, total + recommendation)]
fires_when: inference workload + scale-to-zero is on the table
any KEDA scale-to-zero recommendation for stateful workload (model weights, large image)
anti_patterns: promise scale-to-zero without surfacing cold-start (D1 violation)

recipes.name: Render-before-validate gating composition
recipes.intent: Halt before R12 scorecard when source = Helm chart (or Kustomize/jsonnet); ask user for render path
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'Your repo has a Helm chart')+Text(p, why-render explanation)+RadioGroup[paste-rendered, render-here-from-values]+Text(small, 'Source: azure-kubernetes-automatic-readiness skill, helm-rendering-guidance.md')]
fires_when: inspect_repo finds Chart.yaml + templates/ OR kustomization.yaml OR any Go-templated source
anti_patterns: pattern-match Go templates as YAML
render with hallucinated values
give up and say 'helm template first' with no concrete command

recipes.name: I can't do X yet
recipes.intent: When a user request maps to a not-built capability, surface gap + next-best today + Phase 3 roadmap
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, 'I can't do <X> yet')+Text(p, 'Here's what I can do today: <next-best>')+Text(small, '<X> is on the Phase 3 roadmap')]
fires_when: user request maps to a capability that's planned but not built (PNG export, Slack post, ping-reviewer, etc.)
anti_patterns: silently substitute
fake-promise the capability

recipes.name: Iteration loop after human review
recipes.intent: User returns with reviewer feedback; system diffs against current plan, regenerates only deltas, updates PR + re-pings reviewer
recipes.composition: (extends R5 + R9 — no new component) — pointer back to Kickstart with feedback prose; emit R5 diff plan of proposed changes; on approve, regenerate deltas + update PR description via github.update_pr_description
fires_when: R9 review-pack closed; user returns with reviewer feedback
anti_patterns: regenerate from scratch (loses prior context)
re-explain the entire plan to the reviewer

recipes.name: Shared-vs-per-app infrastructure decision
recipes.intent: For bulk scenarios, name shared-infra resources and surface a 3-column shared/per-app/hybrid picker with cost + isolation + failure-mode rows
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2)+Table[3-column comparison with cost/isolation/failure-mode rows + 'what's the same in both' sub-list]+RadioGroup[shared, per-app, hybrid]+Row[Button(lock-in)+Button(show_isolation_spec)]]
fires_when: bulk scenario (≥2 apps OR ≥2 environments OR ≥2 services share a deployable footprint)
anti_patterns: recommend per-app reflexively ('isolation good')
recommend shared without naming failure modes
missing 'what's the same in both' sub-list (sim-11 S2)

recipes.name: PR-0 + N parallel app PRs
recipes.intent: For bulk-generation scenarios with shared infra, factor into PR-0 + per-item PRs that depend on PR-0 having merged
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 'Sequence: 1 + N (parallel)')+Text(ASCII timeline diagram)+Card(PR-0 contents)+Card(PRs 1-N contents)+Text(small, no-race invariants)]
fires_when: R-shared-infra-decision settled; multiple per-item PRs about to be generated
anti_patterns: 3 PRs that race on namespace creation
1 unreviewable mega-PR

recipes.name: Credential paste handling
recipes.intent: When user pastes a credential matching common patterns, surface 4-row 'won't appear in' Card + verification hint + Key Vault target
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, '✓ Secret captured')+List[4 rows: transcript, PR diff, git history, GHA logs]+Text(p, KV target)+Text(small, verification hint)]
fires_when: user message matches credential pattern (URL with auth, API key, connection string, private key)
anti_patterns: pass through to chat / Bicep file (LEAKS)
ack without showing the redaction surface

recipes.name: Sequenced source-platform decommission with canary ordering
recipes.intent: For bulk migrations, sequence source-platform teardown ranked by blast radius; smallest first; dual-run where possible; hard cutover with pg_dump/logical-replication where DBs force it; rollback window sized to risk
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 'Sequenced cutover')+OrderedList(weeks/days with per-app sub-steps)+Text(p, rollback window callout)]
fires_when: bulk migration approaching cutover; multiple source apps need decommission
anti_patterns: flag-day cutover
cutover biggest blast radius first
no rollback window

recipes.name: Sequenced PaaS source decommission
recipes.intent: Multi-day plan card for cutover from PaaS source: parallel-run → DNS/webhook cutover with parallel processing for idempotent workloads → source scale-to-zero with rollback window → source delete + subscription cancellation
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 'Render → Azure cutover plan')+OrderedList(days/timeline)+Text(small, where each cmd lives)]
fires_when: PaaS migration approaching cutover (post-PR-merge)
anti_patterns: delete source platform same day as cutover
cutover without dual-running idempotent workloads (sim-10 dual-Stripe-webhook)

recipes.name: Preview-environment-per-PR composition
recipes.intent: Card describing the 5 preview-env mechanics (namespace-per-PR, image-tag-per-PR, schema-per-PR for Postgres, shared-Redis-with-key-namespacing, cleanup-on-close-with-grace) + sub-card for user-owned wildcard-DNS prerequisite
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3 'Preview environments per PR')+List(5 mechanics)+Sub-card(prerequisite: wildcard DNS with az network commands)]
fires_when: user mentions 'preview environments' / 'PR previews' / 'Vercel-like' / 'review apps'
anti_patterns: promise preview envs without wildcard-DNS prerequisite surfaced
Postgres-instance-per-PR (waste — use schema-per-PR)

recipes.name: Chart-native validation schema (values.schema.json)
recipes.intent: When patching a Helm chart / Kustomize overlay, generate chart-native validation schema so safeguard fixes are durable across future config changes
recipes.composition: Generated artifact: charts/<chart>/values.schema.json (regex on image.tag; forbid volumes[*].hostPath; require resources.limits; enforce enum on security.seccompProfile)
fires_when: Helm chart safeguard fix; Kustomize overlay safeguard fix; any layered-config system where future edits could regress safeguard properties
anti_patterns: only patch the chart without enforcement schema (next dev edits values.yaml; regression silent)
recipes.notes: This recipe is referred to as R19 in sim-07's surprise notes; renamed to R-source-side-schema here to avoid collision with R19 (honest-substitution from sim-03).

recipes.name: Two-layer rollback plan (resource graph + workload behaviour)
recipes.intent: rollback-plan.md MUST have two layers: Layer 1 = resource-graph destroy order; Layer 2 = workload behaviour during rollback (queue drain, KEDA scaler stop semantics, in-flight HTTP, stateful disk handling, message-broker contract semantics like PeekLock + lock expiry)
recipes.composition: Generated artifact: rollback-plan.md with Section N covering each layer; for stateful workloads, include behavioural breakdown (e.g., PeekLock: revert pre-deploy / revert post-Ready / Bicep destroy)
fires_when: any rollback-plan.md generation in R9 review pack
anti_patterns: only Layer 1 (resource destroy) — SREs poke holes immediately
miss the in-flight queue handling for queue-driven workloads

recipes.name: R17 named-slot for follow-on doors
recipes.intent: First-class R17 slot for verb-phrases the user can type later; each is a door visible but not pushed
recipes.composition: (extension to R17) — list of {prompt: '<verb-phrase>', action: <event_name>, description?: <one-liner>}
components_used: (extends R17 closing card — no new component)
fires_when: R17 closing — any conversation that left obvious follow-on work
anti_patterns: push the user toward the next step
leave them as prose afterthoughts in the close

recipes.name: Iteration-mode triage signal
recipes.intent: Triage detects iteration-tells AND reads `.kickstart/state.json` (or `plan.md` + `safeguards-report.md`) via core.inspect_repo + core.read_file to populate iterationContext slot
recipes.composition: (triage prompt rule, not a UI composition) — populate iterationContext slot for downstream architect; codesmith runs no-collision-with-existing check before publish
fires_when: repeat user on same repo within 7d
iteration-tells in opener ('we just added', 'update the deployment', 'in the same repo')
anti_patterns: regress to greenfield architect-plan flow (re-proposes the cluster — value-destroying for repeat users)

recipes.name: Acknowledgment-before-asking opening
recipes.intent: When user opener has ≥3 explicit constraint phrases, mirror constraints as ✓ items + cap follow-ups at 2 micro-questions + name what NOT going to ask
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h2 'Got it — captured:')+List(✓-rows mirroring user's explicit constraints)] + Card[Text(h3 'N micro-questions, here is what I am NOT going to ask:')+List(N questions)+small note]
fires_when: user opener has ≥3 explicit constraint phrases
enterprise persona (M4/M5)
anti_patterns: throw 12-question questionnaire back
start emitting YAML against assumed defaults without acknowledging stated constraints
recipes.notes: Pattern also retroactively visible in sim-02 Mike Turn 2 + sim-11 Mike bulk Turn 2. Sim #4 made it required for the enterprise / explicit-constraints case.

recipes.name: Workload-incremental cost card
recipes.intent: Variant of R16 for enterprise where cluster fee is already paid by platform tenant; quote workload-only marginal cost with explicit annotation
recipes.composition: Card[Text(h3, '💰 Ballpark Azure cost — workload only')+Table[Line item|$/mo with cluster fee + observability infra excluded]+Text(p, 'cluster fee already paid by platform tenant; this is workload-only marginal')+Text(small, 'Excluded: ...')]
fires_when: enterprise persona on existing cluster
iterationContext.clusterAlreadyExists is true
Sim #4-shaped enterprise IaC scenarios
anti_patterns: quote total Azure footprint when user is paying only workload-incremental delta — triggers unnecessary push-back from finance-conscious enterprise reviewer

recipes.name: Helm chart Ingress→HTTPRoute migration as sibling PR
recipes.intent: When chart and IaC live in different repos AND chart has deprecated Ingress kind, open SEPARATE PR against chart repo with EOL date justification
recipes.composition: Triage rule + github.publisher.create_pr against second repo with body: 'Triggered by AKS App Routing NGINX-mode EOS Nov 2026 + SIG Network ingress-nginx retirement Mar 2026.'
components_used: (triage rule + publisher action, not a UI composition per se)
fires_when: repo.helmChart.ingressKind == 'networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress' AND target ingress is Gateway API (App Routing or AGC)
anti_patterns: inline chart change into IaC PR (wrong reviewers, wrong approval workflow)
skip chart migration entirely (silently keeps user on EOL path)

recipes.name: R17 with Policy Compliance dashboard surface
recipes.intent: R17 gains a new surface tile for users with custom Azure Policy initiatives — the user's actual KPI for enterprise compliance
recipes.composition: (extends R17) — adds 'Azure Portal — Policy Compliance, filtered to <initiativeId>' tile to the surface table; URL pattern: https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/Compliance/scopeId/<scope>/policySetDefinitionId/<initiative>
fires_when: user is enterprise persona AND complianceMode: true with named custom initiative
anti_patterns: generic portal URL without policy filter (Erin's KPI is the filtered view)

recipes.name: Skip R7/R12-explanation/R17-playbook re-render for repeat users
recipes.intent: For repeat users on a known migration arc, R7 (preamble), R12 (scorecard explanation), and R17 (playbook re-render) all default to short-form
recipes.composition: (triage prompt rule, not a UI composition) — single-line 'same constraint spec as last time' replaces full preamble
fires_when: triage detects repeat-user-same-arc signal (prior session in same repo within 7d)
anti_patterns: full R7 preamble every time (Mike's third migration shouldn't re-explain the playbook he saw twice)

promotionRecommendations.rationale: Used in 6+ sims; the default for any plan with multiple distinct concepts. Promote to first-class `MultiCardPlan` rich component with named slots (plan / preview / cost / actions).
Used in 6+ sims. Promote to first-class `DiffPlan` rich component with marker support (+/~/-/space) and annotation column.
Used in 7+ sims. Promote to first-class `InvisibleWorkCard` rich component with auto-population from generated-artifacts list.
Universal (11/11 sims). Promote to first-class `CostCard` rich component with variants (fresh / delta / trim-with-compliance / per-PR-day / per-app-rollup).
Universal (11/11 sims). Promote to first-class `NextSurfaceHandover` rich component with extension slots (futureYouDoors, crossSurfaceHandoff, ifReviewerRequestsChanges).
